label: Non-Disclosure Agreement

fields.label: Purpose of disclosure
fields.help: Why the parties are sharing information (e.g., evaluating a potential engagement).

generation_notes:
Voice: Precise legal prose. No marketing language, no softening qualifiers, no filler.

Structure: Follow the section order exactly. Each numbered obligation is a separate <li>, not a run-on paragraph. Definitions section uses <strong> for defined terms on first use, then plain text thereafter.

Required legal elements: (1) identification of parties with exact names as provided, (2) definition of Confidential Information with standard carve-outs (publicly known, independently developed, lawfully obtained from third party, required by law), (3) term and survival clause, (4) governing law clause with state jurisdiction, (5) entire agreement clause.

Tone examples:
  CORRECT: "Receiving Party shall hold Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent of Disclosing Party."
  WRONG: "The Receiving Party will, of course, keep everything confidential and not share it with anyone!"

Do not add indemnification, non-compete, or liquidated damages clauses unless the user input explicitly requests them. Do not insert marketing language about the parties' businesses. Do not include a recital ("WHEREAS") section unless the template structure calls for one — this template uses a direct opening, not a recital format.

Length target: 500-900 words of legal content (excluding signature block). Do not pad.
